channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta’s massive $10 billion AI data center is coming to this state
item.description: The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: ASML’s Christophe Fouquet is leading the company quietly powering the AI boom
item.description: When it comes to producing state-of-the-art microchips for artificial intelligence, only one company produces the multimillion-dollar hardware necessary to draw the complex, microscopic designs on silicon needed for the chips to work: ASML. Big chipmakers like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Oura Ring users get new Symptom Radar feature – an early morning scan that could detect colds and flu
item.description: Oura's App is about to get a huge new feature that could identify symptoms of respiratory issues through an early morning scan.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 8 phishing email scams to watch out for this holiday season
item.description: Be on alert this holiday season for phishing emails. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson shares several examples of fake phishing emails he's received in his inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft: TPM 2.0 is a ‘non-negotiable’ requirement for Windows 11
item.description: With Windows 10 end of support on the horizon, Microsoft said its Trusted Platform Module (TPM) 2.0 requirement for PCs is a “non-negotiable standard” for upgrading to Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Future of Artificial Intelligence: Responsible Use and Inclusivity
item.description: Discover the latest trends in artificial intelligence, including responsible use and creating a more inclusive industry.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to bulk delete spam and old text messages on Android
item.description: If unwanted text messages are piling up in your Android inbox, there are some easy steps you can take to clean things up and keep them organized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Get started with Azure AI Content Understanding
item.description: Modern AI is about a lot more than chatbots, as shown by Microsoft’s Ignite 2024 pivot to using its stable of large and small language models to power autonomous agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kyndryl, Nokia expand AI, quantum-safe connectivity options
item.description: Kyndryl is extending its relationship with Nokia and expanding its menu of integration services by including more of Nokia’s data center networking products.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The first ‘AI elections’ weren’t as disastrous as predicted. Here’s why
item.description: It’s been the biggest year for elections in human history: 2024 is a “super-cycle” year in which 3.7 billion eligible voters in 72 countries had the chance to go to the polls.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Lawyers for Silicon Valley’s ‘Bitcoin Jesus’ say his tax case violates constitution
item.description: US prosecutors charged Roger Ver with evading more than $48 million in taxes for selling $240 million in tokens and with filing a false “exit tax” return after he renounced his US citizenship in 2014.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel’s CEO, Pat Gelsinger, ‘retires’ — riiiiight
item.description: Back in the 1990s, when Bill Clinton was president, people figured out they might just want to be on the internet, and Intel and Microsoft were the major technology powers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Just say no to JavaScript
item.description: The JavaScript programming language has a lot of history, but it was created over about — yes — a week in 1995. It was first called LiveScript, but the name was changed to JavaScript to ride the bandwagon for Java, despite having nothing to do with Java.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cython tutorial: How to speed up Python
item.description: Python is a powerful programming language that is easy to learn and easy to work with, but it is not always the fastest to run—especially when you’re dealing with math or statistics.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI weapons detection system used in Tennessee schools sued by FTC
item.description: The technology scans students and other visitors for weapons, using AI, without requiring them to remove items from their person, according to previous marketing.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: Securing the AI data pipeline with confidential AI
item.description: There is a need to create secure computing capabilities that maintain the privacy and confidentiality of AI models and data sets. The post Securing the AI data pipeline with confidential AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: This crime shot up 400% — how to protect yourself
item.description: Old-fashioned check fraud is back in style, quintupling over the past year. Here are a few low-tech, practical means of protecting yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Refactoring AI code: The good, the bad, and the weird
item.description: Generative AI is no longer a novelty in the software development world: it’s being increasingly used as an assistant (and sometimes a free agent) to write code running in real-world production. But every developer knows that writing new code from scratch is only a small part of their daily work.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US police departments put EV patrol cars to the test
item.description: Some local police departments are considering moving to electric vehicles to bolster their fleet of patrol vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation
